content.text:
<user_query>
# VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator — Claude Code Prompt

## What to build

Build a full-stack web application that scrapes, normalizes, and aggregates startup portfolio data from VC firms and accelerator websites into a single searchable database. Think "Booking.com for startup portfolios."

## Tech stack

- **Backend**: Python + FastAPI
- **Scraping**: Playwright (for JS-rendered pages) + BeautifulSoup (for static pages)
- **LLM extraction**: Use Claude API (Anthropic SDK) to extract structured data from messy HTML — this is the secret weapon for handling wildly different portfolio page formats without writing a custom parser per site
- **Database**: SQLite with SQLAlchemy ORM (easy to swap to Postgres later)
- **Frontend**: Next.js 14 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- **Scheduling**: APScheduler for periodic re-scraping

## Project structure

```
vc-aggregator/
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py                  # FastAPI app entry point
│   ├── config.py                # Settings, API keys, DB path
│   ├── database/
│   │   ├── models.py            # SQLAlchemy models
│   │   ├── connection.py        # DB engine + session
│   │   └── seed_sources.py      # Seed list of VC/accelerator URLs
│   ├── scraper/
│   │   ├── engine.py            # Core scraping logic (Playwright + BS4)
│   │   ├── extractor.py         # Claude API-powered data extraction
│   │   ├── normalizer.py        # Clean + deduplicate data
│   │   └── scheduler.py         # APScheduler cron jobs
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── routes.py            # REST endpoints
│   │   └── schemas.py           # Pydantic request/response models
│   └── requirements.txt
├── frontend/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── page.tsx             # Main search/browse page
│   │   ├── company/[id]/page.tsx # Company detail page
│   │   ├── investors/page.tsx   # Browse by investor
│   │   └── layout.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── SearchBar.tsx
│   │   ├── CompanyCard.tsx
│   │   ├── FilterSidebar.tsx
│   │   ├── InvestorGrid.tsx
│   │   └── DataTable.tsx
│   └── package.json
└── README.md
```

## Database schema

```sql
-- The investors (VCs, accelerators)
CREATE TABLE investors (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- "Sequoia Capital"
    type TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- "vc" | "accelerator" | "angel" | "corporate"
    website TEXT,                          -- "https://sequoiacap.com"
    portfolio_url TEXT NOT NULL,           -- direct URL to their portfolio page
    logo_url TEXT,
    description TEXT,
    location TEXT,
    last_scraped_at TIMESTAMP,
    scrape_status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',  -- "pending" | "success" | "failed"
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- The startups/companies in portfolios
CREATE TABLE companies (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    slug TEXT UNIQUE,                      -- URL-friendly name
    website TEXT,
    description TEXT,                      -- one-liner or short description
    long_description TEXT,
    sector TEXT,                           -- "fintech", "healthtech", "saas", etc.
    stage TEXT,                            -- "pre-seed", "seed", "series-a", etc.
    founded_year INTEGER,
    location TEXT,
    employee_count TEXT,                   -- range like "11-50"
    logo_url TEXT,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',          -- "active" | "acquired" | "dead" | "ipo"
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Many-to-many: which investor backed which company
CREATE TABLE investments (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    investor_id INTEGER REFERENCES investors(id),
    company_id INTEGER REFERENCES companies(id),
    investment_date TEXT,                  -- may be approximate
    round_type TEXT,                       -- "seed", "series-a", etc.
    amount TEXT,                           -- often not public, store as text
    is_lead BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    source_url TEXT,                       -- where we scraped this from
    first_seen_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    last_confirmed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE(investor_id, company_id, round_type)
);

-- Track scraping runs for debugging
CREATE TABLE scrape_logs (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    investor_id INTEGER REFERENCES investors(id),
    started_at TIMESTAMP,
    finished_at TIMESTAMP,
    status TEXT,                           -- "success" | "failed" | "partial"
    companies_found INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    companies_new INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    companies_updated INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    error_message TEXT
);
```

## Seed data — initial list of sources to scrape

Start with these portfolio pages. Store them in a JSON seed file and load into the investors table:

```json
[
    {"name": "Y Combinator", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.ycombinator.com/companies", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Techstars", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.techstars.com/portfolio", "location": "Boulder, CO"},
    {"name": "500 Global", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://500.co/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Sequoia Capital", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.sequoiacap.com/our-companies/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "a16z", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://a16z.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Accel", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.accel.com/portfolio", "location": "Palo Alto, CA"},
    {"name": "Benchmark", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.benchmark.com/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "First Round Capital", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://firstround.com/companies/", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Greylock", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://greylock.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Lightspeed Venture Partners", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://lsvp.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Founders Fund", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://foundersfund.com/portfolio/", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Bessemer Venture Partners", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.bvp.com/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "NEA", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.nea.com/portfolio", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Index Ventures", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.indexventures.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},
    {"name": "GV (Google Ventures)", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.gv.com/portfolio/", "location": "Mountain View, CA"},
    {"name": "Kleiner Perkins", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.kleinerperkins.com/portfolio", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Union Square Ventures", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.usv.com/portfolio/", "location": "New York, NY"},
    {"name": "Antler", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.antler.co/portfolio", "location": "Singapore"},
    {"name": "Entrepreneur First", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.joinef.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},
    {"name": "Plug and Play", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.plugandplaytechcenter.com/portfolio/", "location": "Sunnyvale, CA"}
]
```

## Core scraping logic — the Claude-powered extraction

This is the key innovation. Instead of writing a custom scraper for each site, use Claude to extract structured data from raw HTML. Here's the approach:

### In `scraper/engine.py`:

1. Use Playwright to load the portfolio page (handles JS-rendered content)
2. Wait for the page to fully render (wait for network idle)
3. Extract the page's HTML content
4. If the page is very long or has pagination, handle scrolling/pagination to get all companies
5. Chunk the HTML if it's too large for a single Claude API call (split by logical sections)

### In `scraper/extractor.py`:

Send the raw HTML to Claude API with this system prompt:

```
You are a data extraction engine. You will receive HTML from a venture capital or accelerator portfolio page.

Extract ALL companies/startups listed on this page into a JSON array.

For each company, extract whatever fields are available:
- name (required)
- website_url
- description (short, one-liner if available)
- sector/industry
- stage (pre-seed, seed, series-a, series-b, etc.)
- location
- founded_year
- logo_url
- status (active, acquired, dead, ipo — default to "active" if not specified)

Rules:
- Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown formatting, no explanation
- If a field is not available, omit it (don't set null)
- Normalize sectors to lowercase (e.g., "fintech", "healthtech", "saas", "ai", "devtools", "biotech", "edtech", "climate", "crypto", "ecommerce", "marketplace")
- Normalize stages to: "pre-seed", "seed", "series-a", "series-b", "series-c", "series-d", "growth", "ipo"
- Extract ALL companies, not just featured ones
- If the page has hundreds of companies, extract every single one

Response format:
[
  {"name": "CompanyName", "website_url": "https://...", "description": "...", "sector": "...", ...},
  ...
]
```

Use `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` for cost efficiency. Send the HTML as user message content. Parse the JSON response and pass it to the normalizer.

### In `scraper/normalizer.py`:

1. Fuzzy-match incoming companies against existing DB records (use company name + website for matching)
2. If a company already exists, update fields that were previously empty
3. If a company is new, create it
4. Create the investment link between investor and company
5. Log the scrape run results

## API endpoints

```
GET  /api/companies                    — list/search companies
     ?q=search_term                    — full-text search on name + description
     ?sector=fintech,saas              — filter by sector(s)
     ?stage=seed,series-a              — filter by stage(s)
     ?investor=Y+Combinator            — filter by investor name
     ?location=San+Francisco           — filter by location
     ?sort=newest|name|investors_count  — sort order
     ?page=1&per_page=50               — pagination

GET  /api/companies/:slug              — single company detail + all its investors

GET  /api/investors                    — list all investors
     ?type=vc|accelerator              — filter by type

GET  /api/investors/:id                — single investor detail + full portfolio

GET  /api/stats                        — dashboard stats (total companies, investors, sectors breakdown)

POST /api/scrape/:investor_id          — trigger manual scrape for one investor (admin)
POST /api/scrape/all                   — trigger full scrape cycle (admin)

GET  /api/scrape/logs                  — view recent scrape logs (admin)
```

## Frontend pages

### 1. Main page (`/`)
- Large search bar at top (like Booking.com)
- Quick stats: "Tracking X,XXX startups across XX investors"
- Filter sidebar: sector tags, stage pills, investor checkboxes, location
- Results grid: company cards showing name, one-liner, sector badge, stage badge, investor logos
- Infinite scroll or pagination

### 2. Company detail page (`/company/[slug]`)
- Company name, logo, description
- Key facts: sector, stage, location, founded year, website link
- "Backed by" section showing investor cards
- Similar companies (same sector + stage)

### 3. Investors page (`/investors`)
- Grid of investor cards with logo, name, type, portfolio count
- Click through to see full portfolio

### 4. Investor detail page (`/investors/[id]`)
- Investor info + full portfolio table with search/filter
- Stats: total portfolio companies, sector breakdown (pie chart), stage breakdown

## Design direction

- Clean, minimal, professional. Think Linear or Notion aesthetics
- Dark/light mode toggle
- Use shadcn/ui components: Command palette for search, Badge for tags, Card for companies, DataTable for lists
- Responsive — works on mobile
- Fast — use server components where possible, client components only for interactivity

## Important implementation details

1. **Rate limiting scrapers**: Add 2-3 second delays between page loads. Respect robots.txt. Use random user agents.

2. **Handling pagination**: Many portfolio pages load companies dynamically. Use Playwright's scroll-to-bottom and wait-for-new-content pattern. Some sites (like YC) have their own API endpoints you can discover in the network tab — prefer those.

3. **Deduplication**: Companies appear across multiple investors. Use website domain as primary dedup key, fall back to fuzzy name matching (use `thefuzz` library).

4. **Error handling**: Scraping is inherently flaky. Wrap everything in try/except, log failures per investor, and keep going. A failed scrape for one investor shouldn't block others.

5. **Data freshness**: Store `last_scraped_at` per investor. The scheduler should re-scrape each investor weekly. Show "last updated" dates in the UI.

6. **Environment variables needed**:
   - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — for Claude-powered extraction
   - `DATABASE_URL` — defaults to local SQLite

## Build order

Phase 1 — Get data flowing:
1. Set up the database models and connection
2. Seed the investors table with the URL list
3. Build the scraper engine (Playwright page loader)
4. Build the Claude extractor
5. Build the normalizer + DB writer
6. Test end-to-end with 3-4 investors manually

Phase 2 — Build the API:
1. FastAPI app with all the endpoints above
2. Full-text search using SQLite FTS5
3. Test with curl/httpie

Phase 3 — Build the frontend:
1. Next.js app with the search page
2. Company cards + filter sidebar
3. Company detail page
4. Investors browsing page
5. Connect everything to the API

Phase 4 — Polish:
1. Add the scheduler for automated re-scraping
2. Add scrape logs viewer
3. Add loading states, error states, empty states
4. Dark mode
5. Deploy instructions (Docker compose)
</user_query>

I’m going to scaffold this as a new `vc-aggregator/` app in your repo, then implement backend first (DB, scraper pipeline, API, scheduler), followed by a minimal but functional Next.js frontend wired to the API. I’ll start by checking the current workspace structure so I can avoid interfering with your existing `habitat-site` files.

I found a clean workspace with your existing `habitat-site` project, so I’ll create a separate `vc-aggregator/` folder and keep everything isolated. Next I’m creating the backend scaffold and core implementation files end-to-end before wiring the frontend pages.
I’m now implementing the seed source dataset and the normalization/scrape pipeline so you can run end-to-end ingestion quickly after installing dependencies.
Backend scraping + normalization is in place; I’m now wiring FastAPI endpoints (including filtering, stats, and scrape triggers) and database initialization so this can run immediately.
I’m moving to the frontend now: creating a minimal Next.js 14 App Router UI with search, filters, company detail, investors list/detail, and API integration against the FastAPI backend.

I’ve finished the initial full-stack scaffold and I’m now validating it with lint/type checks and then I’ll add a README with run instructions plus implementation notes (scheduler, scraping behavior, env vars).

Implemented a full `vc-aggregator/` project scaffold with working backend + frontend structure, API routes, scraping pipeline, dedup/normalization, scheduler, seeds, and local run docs.

### What I added

- **New app root:** `vc-aggregator/`
- **Backend (FastAPI):**
  - `backend/main.py` app entry + startup init
  - `backend/config.py` environment settings
  - `backend/database/connection.py` SQLAlchemy engine/session
  - `backend/database/models.py` tables for `investors`, `companies`, `investments`, `scrape_logs`
  - `backend/database/seed_sources.py` seed loader
  - `backend/data/investor_seeds.json` with your full initial source list
  - `backend/scraper/engine.py` Playwright loader + scroll + chunking + robots check + UA rotation
  - `backend/scraper/extractor.py` Claude extraction (`claude-sonnet-4-20250514`)
  - `backend/scraper/normalizer.py` dedup (domain + fuzzy name), upsert, investment linking, scrape logs
  - `backend/scraper/service.py` single/all scrape orchestration
  - `backend/scraper/scheduler.py` weekly APScheduler job
  - `backend/api/routes.py` query/filter/stats/scrape route logic (+ FTS table bootstrap)
  - `backend/api/schemas.py` response schemas
  - `backend/requirements.txt`
- **Frontend (Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind):**
  - `frontend/app/page.tsx` search/browse home
  - `frontend/app/company/[slug]/page.tsx` company detail
  - `frontend/app/investors/page.tsx` investor grid
  - `frontend/app/investors/[id]/page.tsx` investor detail + portfolio table
  - `frontend/app/layout.tsx`, `frontend/app/globals.css`
  - Components:
    - `frontend/components/SearchBar.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/CompanyCard.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/FilterSidebar.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/InvestorGrid.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/DataTable.tsx`
    - `frontend/components/types.ts`
  - Config:
    - `frontend/package.json`
    - `frontend/tsconfig.json`
    - `frontend/tailwind.config.ts`
    - `frontend/postcss.config.mjs`
    - `frontend/next.config.mjs`
    - `frontend/next-env.d.ts`
- **Ops/docs:**
  - `vc-aggregator/.env.example`
  - `vc-aggregator/docker-compose.yml`
  - `vc-aggregator/README.md`

### Validation run

- Python syntax check passed for backend modules (`py_compile`).
- IDE lints reported no issues on edited backend/frontend paths.

### Important notes

- FTS setup is initialized (`companies_fts`) and used in search with fallback to `LIKE`.
- Shadcn/ui-specific components are represented with clean Tailwind primitives (no generated shadcn registry install yet).
- I didn’t run live scraping/API/frontend runtime because dependencies/install steps weren’t executed in this pass.

### How to run

From `vc-aggregator/`:

1. `cp .env.example .env` and set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
2. Backend:
   - `cd backend`
   - `pip install -r requirements.txt`
   - `playwright install chromium`
   - `uvicorn main:app --reload`
3. Frontend:
   - `cd ../frontend`
   - `npm install`
   - `npm run dev`

If you want, I can do the next pass to:
- fully wire **true shadcn/ui components**,
- add **investor logos + similar companies section + chart breakdowns**,
- and implement **cursor-based infinite scroll** on the main page.

<user_query>
yeah go ahead i want to really see it
</user_query>

Perfect — I’ll now make it feel real end-to-end: add richer UI (charts, similar companies, better filters), seed demo portfolio data so the screens aren’t empty, and then actually boot backend + frontend so you can open and test it locally.

I spotted the main blocker for “seeing it” quickly: empty company data until real scraping succeeds. I’m adding a demo data seeder (safe, only when DB has no companies) plus richer response fields for similar companies and investor breakdown charts.

I’ve added the richer UI/data plumbing. Next step is to actually run both apps so you can open them now; I’ll install dependencies, launch backend on `8000` and frontend on `3000`, and verify the health endpoints.

Live now — both servers are running and you can open the app immediately:

- Frontend: `http://localhost:3000`
- Backend docs: `http://localhost:8000/docs`
- Backend health: `http://localhost:8000/health`

What I upgraded so you can really see it:

- Added **demo seeded companies + investments** (auto-seeds only if no companies exist), so UI isn’t empty.
- Added **company similar-companies** section on `/company/[slug]`.
- Added **investor sector/stage breakdowns** on `/investors/[id]` with pie charts.
- Improved main page with result count + sort links.
- Added project-level `.gitignore` so generated artifacts stay out of git.
- Installed backend/frontend dependencies and started both apps.

Backend confirmed with data:

- `/api/stats` currently returns `6` companies across `20` investors.

If you want next, I can do a final polish pass for:
- true shadcn/ui component setup (not just Tailwind primitives),
- command palette search UX,
- and infinite scrolling with proper API pagination controls.
